from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triples about an astronaut (or related entities) into a
    single, readable sentence.  The function uses a small set of handcrafted
    templates for the supported predicates and joins the resulting clauses
    with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Basic templates for the most common astronaut predicates.
    # Each template receives (subject, object) and returns a clause string.
    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from objects.
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip().strip('\"')

    templates = {
        "almaMater": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} graduated from {_clean(o)}",
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was born on {_clean(o)}",
        "birthName": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was originally named {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "dateOfRetirement": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} retired in {_clean(o)}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} worked as a {_clean(o)}",
        "status": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} is {_clean(o).lower()}",
        "timeInSpace": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} spent {_clean(o)} minutes in space",
        "mission": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} served on the {_clean(o)} mission",
        "selectedByNasa": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was selected by NASA in {_clean(o)}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} died on {_clean(o)}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} is a {_clean(o)} citizen",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} served as chief of the Astronaut Office in {_clean(o)}",
        "title": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} held the title \"{_clean(o)}\"",
        "ribbonAward": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} received the {_clean(o)} ribbon",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was operated by {_clean(o)}",
        "backupPilot": lambda s, o: f"with {_clean(o)} as the backup pilot for {s}",
        "commander": lambda s, o: f"with {_clean(o)} as the commander of {s}",
        "crewMembers": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} was a crew member of {s}",
        "representative": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was represented by {_clean(o)}",
        "alternativeName": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} was also known as {_clean(o)}",
        "awards": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} earned {_clean(o)} awards",
        "award": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} received the award {_clean(o)}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} died in {_clean(o)}",
        # Generic fallback
        "default": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(s)} {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Build clauses without forced subject prefix; rely on templates to include needed subjects.
    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, templates["default"])
        clause = tmpl(triple.subject, triple.object)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence.
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # Use commas between clauses and an "and" before the last clause.
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
